metadata.title: Transformers, Parallel Computation, and Logarithmic Depth

sections.title: Motivation
content: Motivating question:How can transformers be understood as models of parallel computation (e.g. MPC)?
Representational powers of depth?
Multi-step reasoning capabilities?
Comparisons with other models (state-space models, sub-quadratic attention transformers, GNNs)?

sections.title: Transformer Architecture
content: Self-attention unit: f(X) = softmax(XQKᵀXᵀ)XV
Multi-headed attention: L(X) = X + Σ h_i(X)
Arbitrary element-wise encoding (MLP + positional encoding)
Full transformer: T(X) = (φ_D ∘ L_D ∘ ... ∘ L_1 ∘ φ_0)(X)

sections.title: Massively Parallel Computation (MPC)
content: Theoretical model of MapReduce by Karloff, et al '10.
N-bit input divided among q machines with local memory s.
For round r = 1, ..., R: Each machine performs computation and communicates.
(γ, δ)-MPC protocols have local memory s = O(N^δ) and global memory qs = O(N^{1+γ}).

sections.title: Conjecture (1-cycle vs 2-cycle; Ghaffari, et al '19)
content: Any (γ, δ)-MPC protocol with δ ∈ (0, 1) that distinguishes one length-N cycle from two length-N/2 cycles uses R = Ω(log N) rounds.

sections.title: k-Hop Induction Heads
content: Induction Heads:Toy task to complete bigram via previous occurrence: IH(baebcabebdea) = b.
Occurs naturally in trained transformers; useful primitive for mechanistic interpretability.
Two-step logical reasoning task with natural two-layer transformer construction.
k-hop Induction Heads:Iterated variant of IH to capture k-step reasoning.

sections.title: Theory Result #1: Near-equivalence of Transformers & MPC
Theory Result #2: Efficient Multi-Step Reasoning Representations
content: Construction:Transformers of depth L = ⌊log₂ k⌋ + 2 and width m = O(1) can solve k-IH.
First two layers solve induction heads for each input.
Subsequent layers employ "pointer doubling" to chain together solutions.

sections.title: Theory Result #3: Sub-optimality of Other Architectures
content: Graph Neural Networks:Solving graph connectivity requires L√m = Õ(N^{1/4}).
State-Space Models:RNN, LSTM, Mamba solutions to k-IH require depth L ≥ k or width m = Ω(N/k⁶).
Sub-Quadratic Attention Transformers:Every kernel-based or masking-based transformer variant that solves k-IH has either depth L ≥ k or near-quadratic computation.

sections.title: Empirical Result #1: Log-depth k-Hop Induction Heads
content: Task:Multi-task k-IH for k = 0, ..., 16; sequence length N = 100.
Result:Low error when L ≤ ⌊log₂ k⌋, as predicted by theory.

figures.description: Line graph showing classification error vs k for different transformer depths L, demonstrating that error drops as depth increases logarithmically with k.

sections.title: Empirical Result #2: Interpretability of Learned Models
content: 16-IH attention matriceswith colored partial solutions.
Yes.Close correspondence between self-attention matrices and partial solutions to k-IH.

figures.description: Heatmap showing the correspondence between empirical attention matrices and theoretical j-IH maps across layers and heads.
